attributes.readMe:
# Table

## Summary

Table models one seatable unit on the seating-plan canvas — a single banquet table or a whole `section` grid of individual seats. Its schema covers identity and seating configuration (`name`, a `shape` enum of round/oval/rect/square/curved/section/seat, `seatCount`, a `seatingStyle` enum of around/opposite/top/bottom/left/right, plus `rows`, `cols`, and a `seatOrder` enum used by section grids), canvas geometry (`x`, `y`, `width`, `height`, `rotation`, `z` — all numbers), and assignment state (`themeColor`, `reservedCategories` as a containsMany of guest-category values, `seatedGuests` as a linksToMany of Guest cards packed alongside `seatSlots`, plus `vip`, `rank`, `locked`, and `note`). Computed fields derive `seatedCount` from the seated guests and `title` from the name. The planner holds tables in a containsMany field, so each table carries its own seat assignments inside the plan.

## Import

```js
import { Table } from '../table';
```

## Usage as a Field

```ts
@field tables = containsMany(Table);
```

## Template Usage

```handlebars
{{#each @model.tables as |table|}}
  {{table.name}} — {{table.seatedCount}}/{{table.seatCount}} seated
{{/each}}
```

Each entry also renders with its own embedded and fitted views via `<@fields.tables />`.
